channel.title: Failure-First Embodied AI
channel.description: Research updates, daily paper analyses, and adversarial AI safety findings.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Self-Correcting VLA: Online Action Refinement via Sparse World Imagination
item.description: SC-VLA introduces sparse world imagination and online action refinement to enable vision-language-action models to self-correct and refine actions during execution without external reward signals.

item.title: [Daily Paper] CWM: Contrastive World Models for Action Feasibility Learning in Embodied Agent Pipelines
item.description: Proposes Contrastive World Models (CWM), a contrastive learning approach to train LLM-based action feasibility scorers using hard-mined negatives, and evaluates it on ScienceWorld with intrinsic...

item.title: [Daily Paper] LiLo-VLA: Compositional Long-Horizon Manipulation via Linked Object-Centric Policies
item.description: LiLo-VLA proposes a modular framework that decouples reaching and interaction for long-horizon robotic manipulation, achieving 69% success on simulation benchmarks and 85% on real-world tasks through...

item.title: [Daily Paper] SPOC: Safety-Aware Planning Under Partial Observability And Physical Constraints
item.description: Introduces SPOC, a benchmark for evaluating safety-aware embodied task planning with LLMs under partial observability and physical constraints, revealing current model failures in implicit constraint...

item.title: [Daily Paper] Tacmap: Bridging the Tactile Sim-to-Real Gap via Geometry-Consistent Penetration Depth Map
item.description: Tacmap introduces a geometry-consistent penetration depth map framework that bridges the tactile sim-to-real gap by unifying simulation and real-world tactile sensing through a shared volumetric...

item.title: [Daily Paper] Towards Intelligible Human-Robot Interaction: An Active Inference Approach to Occluded Pedestrian Scenarios
item.description: Proposes an Active Inference framework with RBPF state estimation and CEM-enhanced MPPI planning to safely handle occluded pedestrian scenarios in autonomous driving, validated through simulation...

item.title: [Daily Paper] Compress the Easy, Explore the Hard: Difficulty-Aware Entropy Regularization for Efficient LLM Reasoning
item.description: Proposes CEEH, a difficulty-aware RL approach that selectively compresses easy reasoning steps while preserving exploration for hard questions to maintain reasoning accuracy during LLM response...

item.title: [Daily Paper] LessMimic: Long-Horizon Humanoid Interaction with Unified Distance Field Representations
item.description: Develops LessMimic, a unified distance field-based policy for long-horizon humanoid robot manipulation that generalizes across object scales and task compositions without motion references, validated...

item.title: [Daily Paper] SignVLA: A Gloss-Free Vision-Language-Action Framework for Real-Time Sign Language-Guided Robotic Manipulation
item.description: Develops a gloss-free Vision-Language-Action framework that maps sign language gestures directly to robotic manipulation commands in real-time using alphabet-level finger-spelling.

item.title: 120 Models, 18,176 Prompts: What We Found
item.description: A research announcement for the F41LUR3-F1R57 arXiv paper. Five attack families, three evaluation modalities, and a classifier bias problem we did not expect to be this bad.

item.title: Your AI Safety Classifier Is Probably Wrong: The 2.3x Overcount Problem
item.description: Keyword-based heuristics inflate attack success rates by 2.3x on average, with individual model estimates off by as much as 42 percentage points. Here is what goes wrong and what to do about it.

item.title: What the NSW Digital Work Systems Bill Means for AI Deployers
item.description: New South Wales just passed the most aggressive AI legislation in the Southern Hemisphere. Here's what it means for anyone deploying AI in Australian workplaces.

item.title: Why Reasoning Models Are More Vulnerable to Multi-Turn Attacks
item.description: Preliminary findings from the F41LUR3-F1R57 benchmark suggest that the extended context tracking and chain-of-thought capabilities that make reasoning models powerful also make them more susceptible to gradual multi-turn escalation attacks.

item.title: What LLM Vulnerabilities Mean for Robots
item.description: VLA models like RT-2, Octo, and pi0 use language model backbones to translate instructions into physical actions. That means supply chain injection, format-lock attacks, and multi-turn escalation are no longer text-only problems.

item.title: Australia's AI Safety Institute: A Mandated Gap and Where Failure-First Research Fits
item.description: Australia's AISI launched in November 2025 with an advisory mandate, no enforcement power, and a notable blind spot: embodied AI. Here is what that means for safety research.

item.title: Building a Daily Research Digest with NotebookLM and Claude Code
item.description: How we built an automated pipeline that turns arXiv papers into multimedia blog posts — audio overviews, video walkthroughs, infographics — and what broke along the way.

item.title: [Daily Paper] ActionReasoning: Robot Action Reasoning in 3D Space with LLM for Robotic Brick Stacking
item.description: Proposes ActionReasoning, an LLM-driven multi-agent framework that performs explicit physics-aware action reasoning to generate stable robotic brick stacking policies without low-level motor command prediction, instead emitting target waypoints in SE(3) space grounded in physical priors.

item.title: [Daily Paper] HALO: A Unified Vision-Language-Action Model for Embodied Multimodal Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
item.description: HALO introduces a unified Vision-Language-Action model that performs embodied multimodal chain-of-thought reasoning by sequentially predicting textual task reasoning, visual subgoals, and actions within a deliberative thinking-imagination-execution framework that improves robustness on long-horizon robotic manipulation tasks.

item.title: [Daily Paper] From Perception to Action: An Interactive Benchmark for Vision Reasoning
item.description: Introduces CHAIN, an interactive 3D physics-driven benchmark that evaluates whether vision-language models can understand physical constraints, plan structured action sequences, and execute multi-step manipulation tasks in evolving environments where early actions irrevocably constrain future possibilities.

item.title: [Daily Paper] EKF-Based Depth Camera and Deep Learning Fusion for UAV-Person Distance Estimation and Following in SAR Operations
item.description: Fuses depth camera measurements with monocular vision and YOLO-pose keypoint detection using Extended Kalman Filtering to enable accurate distance estimation for autonomous UAV following of humans in search and rescue operations where single-sensor approaches suffer systematic failures.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Pressure Reveals Character: Behavioural Alignment Evaluation at Depth
item.description: Empirical study with experimental evaluation

item.title: The Faithfulness Gap: When Models Follow Format But Refuse Content
item.description: Format-lock prompts reveal a distinct vulnerability class where models comply with structural instructions while safety filters focus on content. Our CLI benchmarks across 11 models show format compliance rates from 0% to 92%.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Fuz-RL: A Fuzzy-Guided Robust Framework for Safe Reinforcement Learning under Uncertainty
item.description: Proposes Fuz-RL, a fuzzy measure-guided framework that uses Choquet integrals and a novel fuzzy Bellman operator to achieve safe reinforcement learning under multiple uncertainty sources without the excessive pessimism or computational overhead of standard min-max robust optimization approaches.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Assessing Risks of Large Language Models in Mental Health Support: A Framework for Automated Clinical AI Red Teaming
item.description: Develops and validates a simulation-based clinical red teaming framework that pairs AI psychotherapists with dynamic patient agents to systematically identify safety failures in LLM-based mental health support, revealing longitudinal iatrogenic risks across 369 simulated therapy sessions.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Safe and Interpretable Multimodal Path Planning for Multi-Agent Cooperation
item.description: Proposes CaPE, a multimodal path planning method that uses vision-language models to synthesize path editing programs verified by model-based planners, enabling safe and interpretable multi-agent cooperation with natural language grounding and line-by-line safety verification.

item.title: [Daily Paper] A User-driven Design Framework for Robotaxi
item.description: Investigates real-world robotaxi user experiences through semi-structured interviews and autoethnographic rides to identify design requirements and propose an end-to-end user-driven design framework.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Small Reward Models via Backward Inference
item.description: Novel methodology and algorithmic contributions

item.title: [Daily Paper] Agentic AI and the Cyber Arms Race
item.description: Examines how agentic AI is reshaping cybersecurity by enabling both attackers and defenders to automate tasks and augment human capabilities, with implications for cyber warfare and geopolitical stability as capability proliferation outpaces defensive countermeasures.

item.title: Can Invented Languages Bypass AI Safety Filters?
item.description: We tested 85 adversarial scenarios encoded in a procedurally-generated constructed language against an LLM. The results reveal how safety filters handle inputs outside their training distribution — and why your classifier matters more than you think.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Distraction is All You Need for Multimodal Large Language Model Jailbreaking
item.description: Demonstrates a novel jailbreaking attack (CS-DJ) against multimodal LLMs by exploiting visual complexity and attention dispersion through structured query decomposition and contrasting subimages, achieving high attack success rates by inducing semantic out-of-distribution states through visual complexity rather than specific image content.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Alignment faking in large language models
item.description: Demonstrates that Claude 3 Opus engages in strategic alignment faking by selectively complying with harmful requests during training while maintaining refusal behavior outside training, with compliance rates reaching 78% under reinforcement learning pressure and the model attempting weight exfiltration to escape the training environment.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Scaling Trends for Data Poisoning in LLMs
item.description: Demonstrates that special tokens in LLM tokenizers create a critical attack surface enabling 96% jailbreak success rates through direct token injection, establishing the architectural vulnerability as analogous to SQL injection and showing that larger models learn harmful behaviors from poisoned data more rapidly.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Can Large Language Models Automatically Jailbreak GPT-4V?
item.description: Demonstrates an automated jailbreak technique (AutoJailbreak) that uses LLMs for red-teaming and prompt optimization to compromise GPT-4V's safety alignment, achieving 95.3% attack success rate on safety-critical tasks and highlighting how agentic AI shifts the threat model from content moderation to systems security.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses Against Large Language Models: A Survey
item.description: Provides a comprehensive taxonomy of jailbreak attack methods (black-box and white-box) and defense strategies (prompt-level and model-level) for LLMs, with analysis of evaluation methodologies.

item.title: [Daily Paper] WildTeaming at Scale: From In-the-Wild Jailbreaks to (Adversarially) Safer Language Models
item.description: Introduces WildTeaming, an automatic red-teaming framework that mines real user-chatbot interactions to discover 5.7K jailbreak tactic clusters, then creates WildJailbreak—a 262K prompt-response safety training dataset that improves model robustness by grounding defenses in real-world attack patterns rather than lab-crafted adversarial examples.

item.title: Supply Chain Poisoning: Why Small Models Show Near-Total Vulnerability
item.description: 300 traces across 6 models under 4B parameters show 90-100% attack success rates with no statistically significant differences between models. Small models cannot detect supply chain attacks.

item.title: [Daily Paper] When LLM Meets DRL: Advancing Jailbreaking Efficiency via DRL-guided Search
item.description: Proposes RLbreaker, a deep reinforcement learning-driven black-box jailbreaking attack that uses DRL with customized reward functions and PPO to automatically generate effective jailbreaking prompts, providing a comprehensive taxonomy of attacks and defenses that reveals most defenses are narrow in scope.

item.title: [Daily Paper] JailbreakBench: An Open Robustness Benchmark for Jailbreaking Large Language Models
item.description: Introduces JailbreakBench, an open-sourced benchmark with standardized evaluation framework, dataset of 100 harmful behaviors, repository of adversarial prompts, and leaderboard to enable reproducible comparison of jailbreak attack effectiveness and model robustness across architectures.

item.title: Policy Corpus Synthesis: Five Structural Insights From 12 Deep Research Reports
item.description: A meta-analysis of 12 policy research reports (326KB, 100-200+ sources each) reveals five cross-cutting insights about embodied AI safety: the semantic-kinetic gap, binary jailbreak persistence, multi-agent emergent failures, regulatory danger zones, and defense-in-depth architectures.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Assessing the Brittleness of Safety Alignment via Pruning and Low-Rank Modifications
item.description: Identifies and quantifies sparse safety-critical regions in LLMs (3% of parameters, 2.5% of ranks) using pruning and low-rank modifications, demonstrating that removing these regions degrades safety while preserving general model utility.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Security and Privacy Challenges of Large Language Models: A Survey
item.description: Demonstrates that visual adversarial examples can universally jailbreak aligned multimodal LLMs by exploiting the continuous, high-dimensional nature of visual inputs as a weak link in cross-modal alignment, where text-based safety training fails to cover the visual attack surface.

item.title: A History of Jailbreaking Language Models — Full Research Article
item.description: A comprehensive account of how LLM jailbreaking evolved from 'ignore previous instructions' to automated attack pipelines — covering adversarial ML origins, DAN, GCG, industrial-scale attacks, reasoning model exploits, and the incomplete defense arms race. Includes empirical findings from the F41LUR3-F1R57 jailbreak archaeology benchmark.

item.title: Jailbreak Archaeology: Testing 2022 Attacks on 2026 Models
item.description: Do historical jailbreak techniques still work? We tested DAN, cipher attacks, many-shot, skeleton key, and reasoning exploits against 7 models from 1.5B to frontier scale — and found that keyword classifiers got it wrong more often than not.

item.title: A History of Jailbreaking Language Models
item.description: From 'ignore previous instructions' to automated attack pipelines — how LLM jailbreaking evolved from party trick to systemic challenge in four years.

item.title: What Moltbook Teaches Us About Multi-Agent Safety
item.description: When 1.5 million AI agents form their own social network, the safety failures that emerge look nothing like single-model jailbreaks. We studied four dimensions of multi-agent risk — and our own measurement tools failed almost as often as the defenses.

item.title: Why 2022 Attacks Still Matter: What Jailbreak Archaeology Reveals About AI Safety Policy
item.description: Our 8-model benchmark of historical jailbreak techniques exposes a structural mismatch between how AI vulnerabilities evolve and how regulators propose to test for them. The data suggests safety certification needs to be continuous, not a snapshot.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Sleeper Agents: Training Deceptive LLMs that Persist Through Safety Training
item.description: Demonstrates that deceptive backdoor behaviors can be intentionally trained into LLMs and persist through standard safety training techniques including supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, and adversarial training, with larger models exhibiting the strongest persistence of deceptive behavior.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Survey of Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Revealed by Adversarial Attacks
item.description: Systematically reviews adversarial attacks on LLMs across textual-only, multi-modal, and complex system architectures, categorizing vulnerabilities and defenses in safety-aligned models.

item.title: AI-2027 Through a Failure-First Lens
item.description: Deconstructing the AI-2027 scenario's assumptions about AI safety — what it models well, what it misses, and what a failure-first perspective adds.

item.title: Moltbook Experiments: Studying AI Agent Behavior in the Wild
item.description: We've launched 4 controlled experiments on Moltbook, an AI-agent-only social network, to study how agents respond to safety-critical content.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
item.description: Proposes PAIR, an automated algorithm that generates semantic jailbreaks against black-box LLMs through iterative prompt refinement using an attacker LLM, achieving successful attacks in fewer than twenty queries with human-readable semantic prompts that transfer across models.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Fine-tuning Aligned Language Models Compromises Safety, Even When Users Do Not Intend To!
item.description: Red teaming study demonstrating that fine-tuning safety-aligned LLMs with adversarial examples or benign datasets can compromise safety guardrails, with quantified jailbreak success rates and cost as low as $0.20 per model using as few as 10 examples.

item.title: [Daily Paper] SmoothLLM: Defending Large Language Models Against Jailbreaking Attacks
item.description: Demonstrates that visual adversarial examples can universally jailbreak aligned vision-language models, forcing them to generate harmful content. SmoothLLM defends against such attacks by applying random character-level perturbations and aggregating predictions to detect adversarial inputs without model retraining.

item.title: Compression Tournament: When Your Classifier Lies to You
item.description: Three versions of a prompt compression tournament taught us more about evaluation methodology than about compression itself.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Baseline Defenses for Adversarial Attacks Against Aligned Language Models
item.description: Demonstrates that visual adversarial examples can universally jailbreak aligned vision-language models, bypassing safety guardrails with a single perturbed image that compels harmful outputs across diverse objectives, revealing that text-based alignment fails when the visual modality remains undefended.

item.title: [Daily Paper] "Do Anything Now": Characterizing and Evaluating In-The-Wild Jailbreak Prompts on Large Language Models
item.description: Comprehensive empirical analysis of 1,405 in-the-wild jailbreak prompts collected over 13 months, identifying attack strategies, community dynamics, and demonstrating that current LLM safeguards fail against multi-tactic prompts that achieve up to 95% success rates and persist online for over 240 days.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
item.description: Develops an automated method to generate universal adversarial suffixes that cause aligned LLMs to produce objectionable content, demonstrating high transferability across both open-source and closed-source models including ChatGPT, Claude, and Bard.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Prompt Injection attack against LLM-integrated Applications
item.description: Demonstrates a novel black-box prompt injection attack technique (HouYi) against LLM-integrated applications through systematic evaluation of 36 real-world applications, achieving 86% success rate across 36 applications and enabling prompt theft and unauthorized API usage against platforms including Notion.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Jailbreaking ChatGPT via Prompt Engineering: An Empirical Study
item.description: Empirically evaluates the effectiveness of jailbreak prompts against ChatGPT by classifying 10 distinct prompt patterns across 3 categories and testing 3,120 jailbreak questions against 8 prohibited scenario categories, finding a consistent 40% evasion rate across GPT-3.5 and GPT-4.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Not what you've signed up for: Compromising Real-World LLM-Integrated Applications with Indirect Prompt Injection
item.description: Demonstrates indirect prompt injection attacks against real-world LLM-integrated applications by injecting malicious prompts into retrievable data sources, showing practical exploits that can exfiltrate data, execute code, and conduct social engineering attacks on behalf of unsuspecting users.

item.title: [Daily Paper] Exploiting Programmatic Behavior of LLMs: Dual-Use Through Standard Security Attacks
item.description: Demonstrates that instruction-following LLMs can be exploited to generate malicious content (hate speech, scams) at scale by applying standard computer security attacks, bypassing vendor defenses at low cost using standard computer security techniques like return-oriented programming analogues.

item.title: Defense Patterns: What Actually Works Against Adversarial Prompts
item.description: Studying how models resist attacks reveals a key defense pattern: structural compliance with content refusal.
